sutta: the buddha said monks be not afraid of deeds of merit it is a name for happiness that is meritorious deeds for well i know monks that deeds of merit done for a long time have a ripening a blossoming which is pleasing joyous and lovely for a long time for 7 years i fostered thoughts of meta loving kindness or goodwill and then for 7 ages of the worlds rolling on and rolling back i came not again to this world then when the world rolled on i reached the sphere of radiance then when the road then when the world rolled back i went to brahma's empty palace then monks i became brahma great brahma the conqueror conquered all seeing all powerful 36 times i was saka devaraja many times 7 was i a wheel turning raja just righteous conquering the 4 ends of the earth bringing stability to the country possessing the 7 gems monks these were my 7 gems the wheel gem the elephant gem the horse gem precious stone gem woman gem house father gem and minister gem and i had more than a thousand suns valiant vigorous crushes of enemy hosts and when i had conquered it i dwelt within this sea boarded country ruling righteously not needing rod or sword that's the end of the suta
commentary: and here the buddha is saying that they should not be afraid of doing good and the buddha said he practiced meditation for 7 years and then he for 7 ages uh or 7 aeons or world cycles he came not again to this world this uh the world rolling on is the growing or the developing of the world and the world rolling back nice the decay of the world is what i have presumed so the world rolled on and rolled back and the world cycle and there are 4 stages like this there is avoid emptiness and then there is the developing of the world the formation of the world that this takes a long time and once the world has formed then it exists like this for a long time and then later it will decay and that decay also takes a long time and after it's finally completely decayed then there's void again so there's 4 things in the cycle in the world cycle then the buddha said now he he went to the sphere of radiance that means uh his meta meditation um in his type of medium meditation he has attained the jhanas ascertained the jana so that he can radiate meta loving kindness a good will to all beings around him that's why he was born in the jhana plains and from there he came down to the brahma became maha brahma great brahma and later that late later after that 36 times he was reborn as saka a deva king and after that when he came back as a human being many many times he was a wheel turning king turning king is a king who rules the whole world so from this suta you find the buddha was talking about his past life and in his past life he had already practiced mata so from the sutas and you find these brahma viharas the 4 things for attaining rebirth in the brahma realm that means kindness then karuna compassion then modita joy and upeka equanimity these were not created by the buddha they were practiced long before the buddha and the next suta is 7.6.59 b this now was 59a this is 59b once the exalted 1 was dwelling near savarti and jetta grove in anathapandi kaspar and early 1 morning after robin himself the exalted 1 taking bowl and glow went to the home of anata pindika the householder and when he had arrived he sat down on the seat made ready now at that time the people of the house were making a great noise in clatter when anatha pindika had come and saluted him and had sat down at 1 side the exalted 1 said why house father do the people of your house make all this din and abroa it is just like fisher meant with a great hall like it seems and anata pindika said lord it is sujata my daughter-in-law who lives with us she is rich and has been brought here from a wealthy family she pays no heat to her mother-in-law not to her father-in-law nor to her husband neither does she venerate honor reverence nor respect the exalted 1 then the exhorted 1 called to her saying come sujatha yes lord she replied and came and saluted the exalted 1 and sat down at 1 side then said the exalted 1 sujata a man may have these 7 kinds of wives what 7 1 like a slayer 1 like a robber 1 like a mistress 1 like my mother 1 like a sister 1 like a companion and 1 like a handmaid these who jata are the 7 and which of them are you and she said lord i do not fully understand what the exalted 1 has put so briefly well word for me lord if the exalted 1 would teach me dhamma so that i may understand the meaning of the words spoken so tersely or so briefly by the exalted 1 and the buddha said then listen i will speak sujata yes lord she replied and the exalted 1 said whoso is peterless corrupted mind neglecting husband and unamiable inflamed by other men a prostitute on murder bent let her be called a slayer and a wife who so she would drop her husband of his gains though little be the profit that he makes whether by craftsmanship or from his trade or by the plow let her be called a robber and a wife the slothful glutton bent on doing nothing a gossip and a shrew with strident voice who brings to low account her husband zeal and industry let her be called a mistress and a wife whoever and with loving sympathy just as a mother for her only son for husband cares and over his stored up wealth keeps watch and ward let her be called a mother and a wife who holds her husband in the same regard as younger sister holds the elder born the meek and heart who in his every wish her husband serves let her be called a sister and a wife and she who is as glad a lot to see as goon companions long apart to me a gracious character of gentle birth a foreign help mate let her be called a companion and a wife if fearless of the lash and stick unmoved all things enduring calm and pure in heart she bear obedience to her husband's word from anger free let her be called a handmaid and a wife now she who is called a mistress slayer thief whose harsh immoral lacking and respect when death comes will wander in the miseries of hell but mother sister or companion slave in precept long established and restrained when death comes will wander in the happy heaven world these who jata are the 7 kinds of wives a man may have and which of them are you and she said lord let the exalted 1 think of me as a handmaid and a wife from this day for that's the end of the sutta so here we find the buddha is trying to teach this young lady to be a good wife and told her all the different types of wife a lady woman can be and so she from that day on she said she wants to be known as a handmaiden and a wife so probably from that day onwards she changed her character the next utah is
